Ad Text:
Data Integration Developer (Azure)


NSW Government Organisation - hybrid Parramatta & WFH - ASAP start; initial 5 month contract - $754 - $879 per day + super

Key Accountabilities

Design, develop and support enterprise integrations using the Azure integration platform, including Logic Apps, API Management, Event Grid and Azure Storage, enabling secure, reliable, and scalable system connectivity.
Lead the development of high-quality, scalable, and secure APIs, embedding best-practice API security controls, performance optimisation, and lifecycle management.
Deliver modern cloud-based integration and services solutions, leveraging microservices architecture to support application, API and data integration across complex enterprise environments.
Strengthen enterprise data integration maturity by developing action plans and reporting to improve data access and governance, maintaining an up-to-date inventory of integration assets, and researching emerging data and integration technologies for practical application
Lead and mentor the integration and services delivery team by providing technical direction, workload planning, design assurance, and peer review, while driving delivery through Agile sprint cycles
Selection Criteria:

Relevant tertiary-level qualifications (e.g. Information Technology, Computer Science, Management Information Systems, or a related discipline)
Proven experience in a similar role
Demonstrated experience automating build, release and deployment pipelines for integration and API solutions.
Hands-on expertise with the Azure integration ecosystem, including Azure Logic Apps, Azure Functions and Azure API Management (APIM).
Strong grounding in API design best practices, including versioning strategies, error handling, data serialisation (JSON, XML) and integration protocols such as REST, SOAP, OData, FTP and JMS.
Solid understanding of data integration standards, security, governance and classification requirements within complex enterprise environments
If you have the relevant experience, please apply now with your up-to-date CV (in Word format).

About AIM

AIM Defence is a fast-growing deep-tech startup developing high-power laser systems to protect people from weaponised drones. We operate at the intersection of cutting-edge technology and defence, with a focus on practical outcomes, not bureaucracy. We are seeking an experienced Systems Engineer to produce high-quality documentation for defence procurement and international partner integration, ensuring that all outputs are rigorous, accurate, and actionable.

The Role

Reporting to the Co-CEO, you will:

Work with the Project Manager and tech leads to gather, validate, and analyse system data.

Identify gaps in data and collaborate to fill them.

Produce technical documentation for defence integration and international partners.

Work in parallel with engineering teams without disrupting workflows.

Interact with customers and partners as part of integration and delivery projects.

Key Responsibilities

Produce comprehensive technical documentation for defence and partner requirements.

Liaise with tech leads to clarify technical data and ensure accuracy.

Identify missing or inconsistent data and drive corrective action.

Communicate technical concepts clearly to internal and external stakeholders.

Support engineering teams without distracting core development work.

Skills & Experience

Minimum 3-5 years of defence systems engineering experience is mandatory.

Startup experience advantageous.

Tertiary qualification in systems engineering preferred but not required.

Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.

Ability to work autonomously and maintain focus on outcomes.

Requirements

Applicants must be Australian Citizens.

Applicants must be able to acquire an AGSVA NV1 Defence Security Clearance

Willingness to work onsite.

Why Join AIM?

Contribute to large-scale defence procurement projects.

Play a key role in documentation and integration with global partners.

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
Talent International is searching for an experienced Data Engineer to join our large mining technology client based in Brisbane CBD on a permanent basis, with 2 days WFH per week.

// Permanent opportunity with a global mining technology leader
// Hybrid work environment - 2 days WFH per week
// Cutting-edge SaaS Data & Analytics platform

The role:
This position will see you play a key role in a large-scale program to develop and deploy a SaaS data and analytics solution for one of the leading mobile mining equipment manufacturers in the APAC region. You'll partner with business stakeholders, technology teams, and third-party ecosystem partners to design data pipeline requirements, data models, algorithms, and predictive models to support operational insights and efficiency.

Responsibilities:

Design and implement new data models and metrics
Assist in establishing the DevOps environment
Develop data pipelines and warehouse solutions in collaboration with technology partners
Collect, prepare, and ensure quality of data across multiple sources
Apply statistical and machine learning techniques to analyse large datasets
Build predictive models to optimise mining operations and equipment maintenance
Develop visualisations, dashboards, and reports for real-time monitoring
Work with engineers and mining experts to improve operational efficiency

Requirements:
2-3+ years of experience in a Data Engineering role
Strong SQL skills and experience with Snowflake, Airflow for data orchestration, and Data Build Tool (DBT)
Proven experience designing and implementing high-performance data pipelines on cloud platforms (AWS preferred, Azure considered)
Hands-on experience with DevOps practices (highly regarded)
Proficiency in Python, R, SQL, Java, or JavaScript
Experience with BI tools such as Looker, Power BI, or Tableau (highly regarded)
Exposure to machine learning techniques (highly regarded)
Experience with Snowflake, Airflow, and AWS (EC2, S3) desirable
Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or related field

How to Apply:

About Aurizn

Aurizn is an Australian-owned defence company delivering capability across the full mission lifecycle in support of a more secure future. For more than 20 years, Aurizn has been a trusted partner to Defence, national security agencies and industry, providing deep technical expertise and operational insight to complex missions.

With a workforce of more than 200 people across Australia, Aurizn delivers Technical and Operational Services, Innovative Capability, and Mission Solutions to domestic and allied customers. The company is backed by private investment and is committed to strengthening Australia’s sovereign capability.

Aurizn has invested more than $2 million in advanced modelling and simulation, AI and ML capabilities, enabling the Aurizn to develop technologies in demand by Defence customers.

We push the limits for a more secure future.

About the Role

Reporting to the Chief Business Development Officer, the Capture and Solutions Architect will be a driving force in shaping and capturing new business opportunities for Aurizn. This role will work with a range of internal and external stakeholders to define delivery approaches, commercial model, programmatic and technical solutioning, and ensure Aurizn’s offering is aligned with customer operational needs and program objectives.

Key Responsibilities: 

Identify and shape strategic opportunities and develop capture strategies aligned with business priorities.
Lead the design and architecture of customer solutions including programmatic delivery, technical solution, supply chain and commercial model.
Coordinate internal stakeholders to develop innovative, competitive offerings.
Support the development of prototypes or demonstration solutions where required to support strategic capture efforts.
Manage and coordinate end-to-end capture activities, including proposal and bid development.
Mentor Aurizn team members in opportunity shaping, solution development and producing compelling proposals.
Collaborate with senior leadership to progress strategic opportunities inline with future strategic plans.

About You:

Ability to obtain a NV1 security clearance is required.   
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or higher.
A minimum of 10 years of previous experience in an engineering/technical role working in the Defence industry.
Minimum experience of 2 years as a Solutions Architect the Growth function in the Defence industry.  Experience across the broad defence acquisition lifecycle from early-stage technology development, to capability design and delivery through to sustainment.
Demonstrated proficiency in capture management techniques and processes such as Shipleys, and the ability to tailor the processes to suit the maturity of the business as it grows. 
Ability to quickly understand technologies including AI/ML, DevSecOps, MLOps, network security, and various physics and parametric modelling techniques.
Exceptional written and verbal communication, and strong stakeholder management skills.

Job Description

SS&C GIDS provides information processing and computer software services and products.  The Company’s operating segments include financial markets, customer management, professional services, and output solutions.  SS&C GIDS serves the alternative investments, asset and wealth management, banking and lending, insurance, and real estate industries.

Extra Perks: Discounts on fitness clubs, travel and more!  

What You Will Get to Do: 

Incident response for production hosted environments 

Monitor application and infrastructure performance to ensure stability and availability.  

Respond to and resolve production incidents quickly, performing root cause analysis where necessary.

Communicate effectively with development, operations, and business teams to provide updates and coordinate resolutions.

What You Will Bring:  

IT degree, or equivalent experience 

4+ years’ experience working in an Application Support role 

Solid understanding of DevOps practices and methods 

Working knowledge of SDLC principles and methodologies 

Experience working with ITIL based processes

Advanced knowledge of Continuous Integration / Continuous Delivery build pipelines

A passion for automating manual processes
